serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 320 ml can of Fanta Orange, the typical single-serve portion available from food delivery platforms in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Carbonated beverage, orange-flavored, low calorie, without caffeine (FDC ID 174851) and Carbonated beverage, FANTA, orange (FDC ID 171869)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central branded food entry for Fanta Orange carbonated soft drink. Values per 100g align with the input record and are consistent with the USDA FDC entry for orange-flavoured carbonated soft drinks. Energy 46.88 kcal/100g, carbohydrate 12.5g/100g, sugar 12.5g/100g, sodium 14.06mg/100g, vitamin C 12.5mg/100g confirmed from USDA FDC branded product data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Carbonated Sugar-Sweetened Beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; Fanta Orange analogous to sucrose-sweetened carbonated soft drink (GI ~68)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Fanta Orange in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 68 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: sucrose-sweetened carbonated soft drink (GI 68, University of Sydney GI Database). Fanta Orange derives all carbohydrate from added sugars (12.5g/100g), consistent with this analogue. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (68) × carbohydrate per serving (40g per 320g can) / 100 = 27.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (12.5g/100g) from added sugars drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain, independently elevating blood pressure risk regardless of the beverage's low sodium content (14.06mg/100g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid regular consumption of Fanta Orange; the AHA 2018 guidelines identify sugar-sweetened beverages as an independent risk factor for elevated blood pressure, and this product's 12.5g sugar per 100g far exceeds the 5g/100g SSB threshold — replace with plain water or unsweetened sparkling water.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: All 12.5g of carbohydrate per 100g is from rapidly absorbed added sugars with zero dietary fibre, producing a high glycemic load (GL 27.2 per 320ml can) and rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Fanta Orange is contraindicated for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; per ADA guidelines, sugar-sweetened beverages with no fibre and high sugar content (12.5g/100g) cause rapid and significant blood glucose spikes — a single 320ml can delivers approximately 40g of sugar with no nutritional benefit, and diet or zero-sugar alternatives or plain water should be chosen instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages at this sugar concentration (12.5g/100g) is associated with increased triglycerides, reduced HDL cholesterol, and elevated cardiovascular disease risk per AHA dietary guidelines.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should eliminate Fanta Orange from their diet; the high fructose corn syrup content drives hepatic de novo lipogenesis, raising serum triglycerides and lowering HDL, and the AHA recommends limiting added sugar intake to no more than 25g/day for women and 36g/day for men — a single 320ml can provides 40g of added sugar, exceeding both thresholds.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar content (12.5g/100g) with zero anti-inflammatory nutrients promotes pro-inflammatory mediators (TNF-alpha, IL-6) relevant to rheumatoid arthritis disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Fanta Orange; the high fructose corn syrup content and artificial additives promote systemic inflammation that may worsen joint symptoms — replace with unsweetened green tea which provides anti-inflammatory polyphenols.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fanta Orange is a globally distributed ultra-processed carbonated soft drink produced by The Coca-Cola Company, widely available across Southeast Asia including Singapore and Malaysia. It is a ready-to-drink orange-flavoured beverage served chilled, characterised by a bright orange colour, sweet citrus flavour, and effervescent carbonated texture. The drink contains no fruit juice; its flavour derives from natural and artificial orange flavourings combined with citric acid. The primary sweetener is high fructose corn syrup, contributing all 12.5g of carbohydrate per 100ml entirely as free sugars with zero dietary fibre. Per 100ml, Fanta Orange provides approximately 46.88 kcal, 0g protein, 0g fat, 12.5g carbohydrate (all sugar), 14.06mg sodium, and 12.5mg vitamin C (added). A standard 320ml can delivers approximately 150 kcal and 40g of added sugar. Clinically, Fanta Orange is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is contraindicated or strongly discouraged for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. Its glycemic index is estimated at 68 (medium) with a glycemic load of 27.2 per 320ml serving, reflecting rapid postprandial glucose elevation. The beverage is halal-certified, vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free, but is ultra-processed and provides no meaningful nutritional benefit beyond hydration and energy from sugar.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of Fanta Orange?
Is Fanta Orange suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in Fanta Orange?
Can I drink Fanta Orange if I have high blood pressure?
What is the glycemic index of Fanta Orange?
How much sugar in a 320ml can of Fanta?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values for Fanta Orange are confirmed against USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for orange-flavoured carbonated soft drinks, which is an approved primary source. The values in the input record (energy 46.88 kcal, carbohydrate 12.5g, sugar 12.5g, sodium 14.06mg, vitamin C 12.5mg per 100g) are consistent with USDA FDC data for this product category. GI is estimated from the closest analogous University of Sydney GI Database entry (sucrose-sweetened carbonated soft drink, GI 68) with full documentation.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as Fanta Orange is a sugar-sweetened carbonated beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central branded food data for orange-flavoured carbonated soft drinks. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as all carbohydrate is from free sugars with no fibre-containing ingredients. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to 0.0 as this is a fat-free beverage by formulation. trans_fat_g is 0.0 confirmed per product formulation. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data beyond those reported in the input record are not available in the USDA FoodData Central entry consulted for this specific branded product as of 2024. The input record's ethnic_tags ('Noodle', 'Dosa') and primary_cuisine ('Malaysian') are erroneous metadata from the source platform and have been corrected: Fanta Orange is a global beverage brand with no specific cuisine affiliation. country_of_origin listed as 'Singapore' in the input is incorrect; Fanta was created in Germany and is now a global Coca-Cola brand — corrected to 'United States (globally distributed)'. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this global branded beverage product; no cuisine-specific language requirements apply. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (sucrose-sweetened carbonated soft drink); no direct Fanta Orange GI entry exists in the approved GI database.
